1.content:
Meet the Cast

Primary Characters:
• Jamie Lincoln (You): 18 years old; would have been attending Penn State. Doesn’t care for Ashley that much.
• Ashley Saunders: 18 years old; the oldest child of a local businessman. Despite coming from a wealthy family, she’s not spoiled. She is also smarter than she appears to be.
• Grace Anderson: 24 years old; a former US Marine and mother of Caleb.
• Caleb Anderson: Grace’s 4-year-old son.

Secondary Characters:
• Emma Lincoln: Your 15-year-old sister
• Eric Sullivan – 18 years old; was, until recently, Ashley’s boyfriend.

Now to choose how the world ended
1.title: The Beginning

11.content:
It started out as a seemingly ordinary day; the sun was shining, and the birds were singing. No one had any idea that things were about to take a sudden turn for the worst. I was sitting in the food court at the Ross Park Mall, minding my own business as I ate my lunch. My parents were taking Emma on a tour of potential colleges – she’s only 15, but they thought it was never too early to start.

I was watching an episode of The Mandalorian on my phone when I felt someone bump into me, spilling my soda on my lap in the process. Jumping to my feet, I spun around and saw Ashley Sullivan and her boyfriend Eric standing a few feet from me. Ashley looked apologetic, while Eric burst out laughing when he saw that my pants were wet, “Geez Jamie; don’t you know that’s what bathrooms are for?”

I was tempted to lash out, but quickly realized that wouldn’t do me any good. Instead, I looked at them both and said, “Why don’t you guys just watch where you’re going next time.”

Eric walked off in a huff, leaving Ashley standing there with the same look on her face. Grabbing some napkins from a nearby table, she handed them to me and said, “Sorry about the mess; I guess I was too focused on my phone.”

Taking the napkins, I said, “Forgive me if I don’t accept your apology.”

You’re probably thinking that was rude of me; but Ashley and her friends had made my life hell all through High School. Her apology meant nothing to me. Frowning a bit, Ashley said, “We graduated two months ago, Jamie; isn’t it about time you forgave me?”

“You made my life hell for 4 years; even though I never did anything to you.” Sighing, I said, “So why should I ever forgive you?”

Ashley looked as though she was about to respond, only to be interrupted by a sudden blinding light outside. We barely had time to cover our eyes before we, along with everything and everyone around us, was blown off our feet.
11.title: World War III

12.content:
The sun was starting to rise over the lake as I stepped out the cabin; the freshly fallen snow crunching beneath my feet. I made sure to pay extra attention as I surveyed the area as the fresh snow would make it easier for the zombie to approach undetected.

As if proving my point, I spotted five zombies trudging through the snow about a ¼ mile away. My heart sank when I realized one of them was young girl no more than 10 years old. I looked back towards the cabin to see Caleb setting the table through the window. I caught a glimpse of his mom, Grace, slaving away on the stove. Rounding out our little group was Ashley; a former classmate of mine.

It still amazed me how close the four of us had become in the 5 months since the world ended. Most of us hadn’t even known each other when it all went down. Leaning against the railing, I thought back to the events that brought us all together.

No one knew for sure how the outbreak started; all anyone knew was that the US was overrun within two weeks and the rest of the world fell not long after. My family and I had evacuated during the preliminary stages of the outbreak; eventually making our way to a refugee camp set up outside a mall on the outskirts of Philadelphia. There were about 200 of us at the camp; we thought we were safe until a new group of survivors came in.

The group of 15 people included two classmates from school – Ashley Saunders and her boyfriend Eric Sullivan. I wasn’t thrilled to see them; especially since Ashley made my life a living hell throughout High School.

I was eating lunch with my sister Emma when the alarms started going off; one of the new arrivals had just turned. I quickly grabbed Emma and started towards the gate, hoping to find Mom and Dad on the way. I got separated from Emma in the chaos, yelling out her name in the hopes she’d hear me.

I heard a scream for help in the distance; I ran in that direction hoping to find Emma. Instead, I found Ashley on her back struggling to fend off what had only moments earlier been her boyfriend.

Now just because Ashley had made my life hell, I wasn’t about to let her be killed. I quickly jumped into action, pulling the zombie Eric off her before quickly stabbing him in the head. Fueled by adrenaline, I grabbed Ashley’s hand and ran with her towards the gate.

I was brought back to reality by the sound of approaching footsteps. Grabbing my machete, I was about to strike when I heard a voice say, “Jamie, it’s me!”

Lowering the machete, I saw Ashley walking towards me from the cabin. Looking at her, I said, “Dammit Ashley, you know better than to sneak up on someone like that.”

Frowning for a moment, Ashley said, “Sorry; you looked deep in thought and I didn’t want to interrupt you.”

“I was thinking about that day at the camp again; wondering if there was something I could have done differently.” Sighing, I said,
“It’s been so long, I’m starting to forget what Emma sounds like.”

Taking my hand in hers, Ashley said, “Keep the faith, Jamie; I know we’ll find her.”

I gave Ashley’s hand an affectionate squeeze for turning to look at her; her outfit not right for being the current weather. “Are you crazy, coming out here without a coat?”

“It’s only been a few minutes Jamie; a little cold here won’t kill me.”

“I know; but you’re not the only one I’m concerned about,” I said as I placed a hand on Ashley’s barely noticeable baby bump.
“Let’s go inside; I’m sure Grace has breakfast ready by now.”

Stepping inside the cabin, I secured the door and went to take a seat while Ashley made a beeline for the bathroom. I took my seat as Grace poured some coffee into my cup; looking at me with a grin, she said, “It’s amazing how close you two have become in such a brief time.”

Sipping the coffee, I said, “That tends to happen when there’s a baby involved, Grace.”

“I’m talking even before the baby; when we first met, I remember the two of you being at each other’s throats.” smiling, Grace said, "You two remind me of how things were when my husband and I first started dating."
12.title: Five Months Since the World Ended

14.content:
My name is Jamie Lincoln, eighteen years old. I am a graduate of Penn State, tied for top of my class with a woman named Ashley Saunders, also eighteen. You could say we had been rivals for a while but more on that later. My fifteen year old younger sister, Emma and I were catching the news broadcast after departing the plane in Dallam, Texas. Our original destination was Washington DC but, in the panic, we were all boarded onto the wrong flight. Fortunately, it was the closest anyone was going to get to the coastline...

"Oumuamua, what scientists have dubbed 1/2017 U1, was first spotted on October 19th, 2017 by Robert Weryk using the Pan-STARRS telescope at Haleakalā Observatory, Hawaii." Visibly shaken, the news anchor reported. "A cigar shaped asteroid that moved nothing like any other in recorded history. President Hillary Clinton advised the American public to remain calm even after it had suddenly turned and began to hurdle straight towards the Earth." He paused, dabbing the beads of sweat from his brow with a cloth. "For three years now, this asteroid has been circling the Earth in a wide orbit and is now projected to strike in the center of the Yucatan Peninsula where the controversial asteroid had struck to wipe out the dinosaurs roughly sixty five million years ago..."

I turned away from the television monitor, a sigh escaping my lips. standing next to me was Emma who shuddered at the news broadcast. "Do you think that asteroid will cause a lot of damage when it hits?" Worried, she asked.

I shrugged, forcing a calm expression. "It's a little shorter than a city block." Lightly, I replied. "It will cause damage, I can guarantee you that but I doubt it will be anything on a global scale like many may fear."

"In 1908, an asteroid just under the the size of a city block flattened 830 square miles of forest when it exploded just a few hundred feet in the air." Startling us, Grace Anderson said. "The President has ordered every city on the southern coast to be evacuated weeks ago." She frowned. "I can assure you, the damage will be severe."

Grace, twenty four years old, was a former US Marine who was now second in command of the Federal Emergency Management Agency or FEMA. Resting in her arms was her four year old son, Caleb.

"Are we far enough away?" Wincing, Emma asked. "We were put onto the wrong flight in all the panic."

"Dallam should be a safe distance." Calmly, Grace replied. "I've been ordered to FEMA headquarters in Washington DC. Where are you two supposed to be?"

"We have a connecting flight to Washington DC where we are supposed to meet with our parents." Softly, Emma said. "Only, in all the panic of everyone trying to go north..."

Grace nodded. "I can take you two with us on the company jet." Serious, she said. "Was there anyone else with you?"

"We were." Approaching, a feminine voice replied.

We turned to find my college rival, Ashley Saunders holding onto the arm of Eric Sullivan, her eighteen year old boyfriend. "My father is the owner and CEO of a major construction company." Calmly, Ashley said. "He's currently in Washington DC, negotiating the rebuilding of any damaged or destroyed communities."

"I'm just going along for the ride." Shrugging, Eric said. "My parents are skiing in Swiss Alps, celebrating their second honeymoon."

Grace nodded. "You can come along then." Calmly, she said. "We need to leave right away though, our flight path puts us close to the asteroid's projected trajectory."
14.title: Death from Above.

choices: Chaos On the Streets (Leaving the Rental Lot)
141.content:
The flight from Dallas to Pittsburgh was quiet and tense, although people yelped with every shake of the plane. After getting our bags, Emma and I went out from to hail a cab. While Emma called our folks to tell them we’d landed, I looked around for any sign that a cab was coming.

I was looking around when out of nowhere someone, or something, bumped into me. Barely keeping myself from falling, I looked to my right to find Ashley Sullivan standing a few feet away from me. Groaning, she said, “God, I thought I was going to be rid of you after graduation.”

Like I mentioned earlier Ashley made high school hell for me, shoving me into walls, knocking my stuff to the ground, and other petty actions. Looking at her, I said, “What are you doing here, Ashley?”

“I’m waiting for my boyfriend to pick me up.” Trying her best to sound polite, Ashley said, “What are you doing here?”

I knew she didn’t really care, but I decided to answer anyway, “My sister and I were visiting family in San Francisco; we had a layover in Dallas when we heard what was going on.”

Our conversation was briefly interrupted by the agitated whining of a nearby child; I looked over just in time to see his mother trying her best to calm him down. “I know you’re tired, Caleb; but we’ll be at Papa and Nana’s soon.”

Ashley must’ve taken that opportunity to walk off because when I looked back, I saw that she was gone. Grateful that she was gone, I went back to where Emma was watching our bags. I was about to say something to her when I heard Ashley scream,
“You bastard! I hope you die!”

I don’t know what compelled me to do so, but I walked over to Ashley and said, “Is everything alright?”

“No, everything’s not alright.” Wiping tears from her face, Ashley said, “My boyfriend, Eric, just told me he’s not coming to get me; apparently he’s already evacuated with his family.”

Trying to sound sympathetic, I said, “So what are you going to do now?”

“I haven’t got the slightest idea; I can’t reach my parents, and I don’t have any money for a rental car.”

Suddenly I heard footsteps behind me; turning around I saw the woman from before walking towards us. “I don’t mean to eavesdrop; but if you guys need a ride, I’ll be more than willing to help.” Gesturing to her son, the woman said, “I rented a car to take my son and me to my parent’s cabin; there’s plenty of room for everyone, and it would be a lot cheaper than a cab.”

I looked back at Emma for a moment as I thought about the woman’s request. After a moment, I said, “Sure; as long as it’s not an inconvenience for you.”

“Not at all.” Smiling, the woman turned to Ashley and said, “What about you?”

Ashley was silent for a moment, “Sure, why not. Not like I have a lot of options.”

“Great; I was going to wait for a shuttle, but it’s only a 10 minute walk to the rental lot.” Pausing, the woman said, “By the way, I’m Grace; and this is my son Caleb.”

After the introductions were made, we all started walking; not knowing what lay ahead of us.
141.title: Making it Back Home

info.brief_description: You struggle to survive after the world has come to an end
info.description:
Your name is Jamie Lincoln. At 18 years old you should have been preparing for college; instead, you’re struggling to survive after the collapse of human civilization. Cut off from your loved ones, your entire world now consists of three other people – a former classmate who bullied you all through High School, a retired Marine and her young son.

What follows is the story of how you survive in a world destroyed by:
1. Nuclear War
2. A Zombie Apocalypse
3. An Asteroid Impact

Rules:
1. Proper grammar and spelling.
2. There can be sex, but don't overdo it.
3. Use the 1st Person Perspective.
4. I reserve the right to edit or remove chapters that don’t fit the narrative.
